title: Pyrus ailanthoides
taxonomy: Pyrus ailanthoides is classified in the kingdom Plantae, and within the kingdom, it belongs to the phylum Magnoliophyta. Further classification places it in the class Magnoliopsida, and more specifically, it is a member of the order Rosales. This species also falls under the family Rosaceae and the genus Pyrus, making it a type of pear tree. The plant is native to East Asia and is also known as the Chinese mountain pear. Within the genus Pyrus, Pyrus ailanthoides is one of many species of pear trees that are commonly found in mountainous regions.
uses: Pyrus ailanthoides, also known as the Chinese mountain ash, is a plant that has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for various purposes. The plant's bark, leaves, and seeds are used to treat conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The root bark of Pyrus ailanthoides is used to treat swelling and inflammation, while its leaves are used to treat fever and cough. The plant's seeds are also used to treat digestive issues and to promote urination. Further research is needed to confirm the effectiveness of these uses, but the plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine.

intro: Pyrus ailanthoides, also known as Ailanthus-pear or Chinese pear, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to China and Southeast Asia, characterized by its broad, ovate leaves and small white flowers. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with potential health benefits including the treatment of fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects. The bioactive constituents of Pyrus ailanthoides include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, the plant is commonly used to make herbal preparations such as decoctions, infusions, and tinctures, often in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
